en.extract:
The purple-tailed imperial pigeon (Ducula rufigaster) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found in New Guinea. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It is sometimes affected by Gunabopicobia parasites.

The '''purple-tailed imperial pigeon''' ('''''Ducula rufigaster''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found in [[New Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It is sometimes affected by Gunabopicobia parasites.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Sikora |first1=Bozena |last2=Unsoeld |first2=Markus |last3=Melzer |first3=Roland R. |last4=Friedrich |first4=Stefan |last5=Skoracki |first5=Maciej |date=2023-04-29 |title=First Records of Picobiine Mites Associated with Birds-of-Paradise: Can Interspecific Sexual Behaviour of Hosts Play a Role in the Distribution of Quill Mite Parasites? |journal=Animals |volume=13 |issue=9 |page=1509 |doi=10.3390/ani13091509 |pmid=37174545 |issn=2076-2615|pmc=10177229 |doi-access=free }}</ref>
